edges.evidence_quote: The PMF trace their origins to the so-called Special Groups, a US term to designate Shiite groups of the Iraqi insurgency, supported and funded by the Iranian Quds Force
edges.context: Quds Force supported and funded the Special Groups that are predecessors of PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: The PMF trace their origins to the so-called Special Groups, a US term to designate Shiite groups of the Iraqi insurgency, supported and funded by the Iranian Quds Force
edges.context: Special Groups were supported and funded by Quds Force.

edges.evidence_quote: Many of its main forces that belong to the Shia faction trace their origins to the 'Special Groups'
edges.context: PMF's main forces trace origins to Special Groups.

edges.evidence_quote: The most powerful groups within the Popular Mobilization Forces are the groups which maintain strong ties with Iran and pledge spiritual allegiance to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. The pro-Khamanei faction would consist of already established parties and of relatively small paramilitaries: Saraya Khurasani, Kata'ib Hezbollah, Kata'ib Abu Fadhl al-Abbas, the Badr Organization and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq.
edges.context: Kata'ib Hezbollah is a component group within the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: The most powerful groups within the Popular Mobilization Forces are the groups which maintain strong ties with Iran and pledge spiritual allegiance to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. edges.context: Badr Organization is a component group within the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: According to Faleh A. Jabar and Renad Mansour, the pro-Sistani faction consists of those armed groups formed by Sistani's fatwa to defend Shia holy sites and by paramilitary of the Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq.
edges.context: Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq is part of the pro-Sistani faction within PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: There are four major groups organized by Najaf: Saraya al-Ataba al-Abbasiya, Saraya al-Ataba al-Hussainiya, Saraya al-Ataba al-Alawiya, and Liwa 'Ali al-Akbar, corresponding to Shia holy sites in Kadhimiya, Karbala, and Najaf.
edges.context: Saraya al-Ataba al-Abbasiya is a component group within the PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: There are four major groups organized by Najaf: Saraya al-Ataba al-Abbasiya, Saraya al-Ataba al-Hussainiya, Saraya al-Ataba al-Alawiya, and Liwa 'Ali al-Akbar, corresponding to Shia holy sites in Kadhimiya, Karbala, and Najaf.
edges.context: Saraya al-Ataba al-Hussainiya is a component group within the PMF.

edges.context: Liwa 'Ali al-Akbar is a component group within the PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: Muqtada al-Sadr 's Peace Companies ( Saraya al-Salam ) were founded in June 2014 from the Mahdi Army.
edges.context: Peace Companies are a component group within the PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: Muqtada al-Sadr 's Peace Companies ( Saraya al-Salam ) were founded in June 2014 from the Mahdi Army.
edges.context: Peace Companies were formed from the Mahdi Army.

edges.evidence_quote: Kata’ib Sayyid al-Shuhada, a member of the PMF, stated that their forces were bombed by US planes on 7 August 2017.
edges.context: Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada is a member group of the PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: Between 2013 and early 2016, 1,200 Iraqi fighters died in Syria, including combatants of pro-Iran militias Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq and Kata'ib al-Imam Ali.
edges.context: Kata'ib al-Imam Ali is a pro-Iran militia within PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2019, U.S. designated Harakat al-Nujaba and its leader Akram al-Ka'abi Specially Designated Global Terrorists (SDGT).
edges.context: Harakat al-Nujaba is a PMF militia designated as terrorist.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 2024, Ansar Allah al-Awfiya was designated as a terrorist organization, following several attacks on U.S. bases in the region including the Tower 22 drone attack.
edges.context: Ansar Allah al-Awfiya is a PMF militia designated as terrorist.

edges.evidence_quote: The Imam Ali Brigades trained two Christian units called Kata’ib Rouh Allah Issa Ibn Miriam (Spirit of God, Jesus Son of Mary Brigade) and the Babylon Brigades.
edges.context: Babylon Brigades are a Christian PMF unit.

edges.target: Kata'ib Rouh Allah Issa Ibn Miriam
edges.evidence_quote: The Imam Ali Brigades trained two Christian units called Kata’ib Rouh Allah Issa Ibn Miriam (Spirit of God, Jesus Son of Mary Brigade) and the Babylon Brigades.
edges.context: Kata'ib Rouh Allah Issa Ibn Miriam is a Christian PMF unit.

edges.evidence_quote: The Imam Ali Brigades trained two Christian units called Kata’ib Rouh Allah Issa Ibn Miriam (Spirit of God, Jesus Son of Mary Brigade) and the Babylon Brigades.
edges.context: Imam Ali Brigades is a PMF unit that trained Christian units.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Badr Organization left the Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq, its leader Ammar al-Hakim formed new paramilitary units, including Saraya el-Jihad, Saraya el-'Aqida, and Saraya 'Ashura.
edges.context: Saraya el-Jihad is a paramilitary unit formed by Ammar al-Hakim, likely part of PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Badr Organization left the Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq, its leader Ammar al-Hakim formed new paramilitary units, including Saraya el-Jihad, Saraya el-'Aqida, and Saraya 'Ashura.
edges.context: Saraya el-'Aqida is a paramilitary unit formed by Ammar al-Hakim, likely part of PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: After the Badr Organization left the Islamic Supreme Council of Iraq, its leader Ammar al-Hakim formed new paramilitary units, including Saraya el-Jihad, Saraya el-'Aqida, and Saraya 'Ashura.
edges.context: Saraya 'Ashura is a paramilitary unit formed by Ammar al-Hakim, likely part of PMF.

edges.evidence_quote: According to Yazan al-Jabouri, a secular Sunni commander of anti-ISIS Liwa Salahaddin, as of November 2016, there were 30,000 Iraqi Sunnis fighting within the ranks of PMUs.
edges.context: Liwa Salahaddin is a Sunni group fighting within PMU ranks.

edges.evidence_quote: The Popular Mobilization Forces have been involved in several battles of the military intervention against the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant since their founding.
edges.context: PMF fought against Islamic State in multiple battles.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2023 there was a brief clash between the Babylon Brigades and the Assyrians of Qaraqosh after the former attempted to take control of a base belonging to the Nineveh Plains Protection Units.
edges.context: Babylon Brigades clashed with Nineveh Plains Protection Units over a base.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 March 2026, the Saraya Awliya al-Dam faction of the IRI published a footage of a reconnaissance drone flying above the US embassy compound in baghdad.
edges.context: Saraya Awliya al-Dam is a faction of the IRI, which is an umbrella for PMF factions.

edges.evidence_quote: On the 21st March 2026 the Ashab al-Kahf faction of the IRI claimed responsibility for killing a French soldier in an attack on a base in Iraq.
edges.context: Ashab al-Kahf is a faction of the IRI, which is an umbrella for PMF factions.

edges.evidence_quote: In the wake of the Mahsa Amini protests, reports surfaced that Iran had brought in Iraqi allies, specifically members of Hashd al-Shaabi and Kata'ib Hezbollah, to assist in cracking down on protests.
edges.context: Kata'ib Hezbollah is a component of the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: Likewise during the 2025–2026 Iranian protests, the presence of Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces, Arabic-speaking mercenaries, Lebanese Hezbollah, and Afghan Liwa Fatemiyoun in suppressing protests was reported.
edges.context: PMF and Lebanese Hezbollah jointly participated in suppressing protests in Iran.

edges.evidence_quote: Likewise during the 2025–2026 Iranian protests, the presence of Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces, Arabic-speaking mercenaries, Lebanese Hezbollah, and Afghan Liwa Fatemiyoun in suppressing protests was reported.
edges.context: PMF and Liwa Fatemiyoun jointly participated in suppressing protests in Iran.

edges.evidence_quote: Approximately 800 members of Iraqi Shia militia groups, including Kata'ib Hezbollah, Harakat al-Nujaba, Sayyid al-Shuhada, and Badr Corps, had been sent to Iran.
edges.context: Harakat al-Nujaba is a component of the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: Approximately 800 members of Iraqi Shia militia groups, including Kata'ib Hezbollah, Harakat al-Nujaba, Sayyid al-Shuhada, and Badr Corps, had been sent to Iran.
edges.context: Sayyid al-Shuhada is a component of the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: Approximately 800 members of Iraqi Shia militia groups, including Kata'ib Hezbollah, Harakat al-Nujaba, Sayyid al-Shuhada, and Badr Corps, had been sent to Iran.
edges.context: Badr Corps is a component of the PMF umbrella.

edges.evidence_quote: Militia leaders insist any abuses are isolated incidents, and target only captured Islamic State's collaborators.
edges.context: PMF militias target Islamic State collaborators.
